question: What does Figure 5.4-7 show about the DHRS configuration, and how do Figures 5.4-8 through 5.4-16 characterize its performance?

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document for regulatory purposes, and any copies must include the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, detailing its structure and topics.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale reactor pressurizer, specifying the types of steel and alloys for each component to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant material specifications from the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and lists the applicable standards for each material used in the pressurizer's construction.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section outlines the testing and inspection requirements for the Reactor Coolant System's high-point vents, specifically focusing on the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components. It details compliance with ASME codes for preservice and inservice inspections, valve classification, and testing procedures, including full-stroke exercising during shutdown and initial in-situ testing for the first reactor module. These measures ensure the DHRS valves and components function properly and meet safety standards.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, plenum access ports, and thermal relief valves, with the RPV itself forming the pressure boundary for the SGs and contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat following reactor shutdown, especially when normal cooling methods are unavailable. It operates without external power, ensuring the reactor coolant system remains below 420°F within 36 hours to maintain fuel and pressure boundary integrity, and its reliability and risk significance are rigorously evaluated. The DHRS does not depend on emergency core cooling system (ECCS) activation but can function alongside ECCS if needed.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design focuses on maintaining proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support plant safety during normal and abnormal conditions. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) contains its own RCS, and the design bases emphasize reliability and safety across all modules.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel over its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that predicted material properties remain within safety criteria throughout the vessel’s operational lifespan.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, and outlines the boundaries between the primary and secondary sides of the system.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials and safety requirements.

edges.description: Section A provides the general context and copyright information for the NuScale Design Certification Application, while Section B introduces the specific components of the reactor coolant system, including the DHRS, thereby specifying the technical scope that the figures in the analysis question address.

Section A is the preface and copyright notice for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, while Section B provides the technical design basis for the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS); thus, Section A structurally references and introduces the context for the detailed technical content found in Section B.

Section A describes the materials and structural components of the pressurizer, while Section B outlines the inspection and testing requirements for DHRS components, connecting them through the shared context of reactor coolant system component design and regulatory compliance.

Section A specifies the materials and components of the pressurizer, which is identified in Section B as one of the key subsystems contained within the reactor pressure vessel alongside the steam generators and DHRS.

Section B describes the design basis and function of the DHRS, which is a subsystem whose components and materials (such as the pressurizer) are detailed in Section A, connecting the general system function to specific component descriptions.

Section B provides a general description of the DHRS as part of the reactor coolant system, while Section A specifies inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, connecting by detailing how the general system elements in B are regulated in A.
Section A specifies the inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, which are part of the system whose safety function and design basis are described in Section B.

Section A identifies the DHRS as a subsystem within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B specifies the DHRS's design basis and functional requirements for removing decay heat during certain events.

Section A provides a technical summary of the reactor coolant system described in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, while Section B is a preface that introduces the same report and establishes its copyright and usage limitations, thereby framing the context for Section A's content.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system, which includes the pressurizer, while Section B gives detailed information about the materials and components of the pressurizer referenced in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's design and reliance on natural circulation, while Section B specifies inspection and testing requirements for DHRS components that are part of this system.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity under various conditions, while Section B provides detailed descriptions of the pressurizer components and their materials, which are relevant to maintaining those integrity limits.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity during transients, while Section B details inspection and testing requirements for DHRS components, both of which are connected by their roles in ensuring safe operation and compliance with technical specifications during plant transients.

Section A provides specific technical details about the DHRS as part of the reactor coolant system, which is a subject covered in the broader context introduced by Section B's preface to Chapter Five.

Section A provides a general overview of the DHRS as part of the reactor coolant system design, while Section B references specific sections for detailed material descriptions of pressurizer components, including those integrated with the RPV mentioned in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the DHRS as part of the reactor coolant system design, while Section B specifies the inspection and testing requirements for DHRS components, thereby detailing how the DHRS must be maintained and verified according to regulatory codes.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's design and reliance on natural circulation, which is the foundational context for Section B's focus on developing operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity under specified operating conditions.
Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system (RCS), while Section B specifies the detailed components and subsystems—including the DHRS—within the RCS described in Section A.

Section A references the need for operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity under transients, while Section B specifies the DHRS as a subsystem within the reactor coolant system that is integral to managing such transients, thus Section B provides technical details for the systems whose operation Section A's procedures must address.

Section A lists references cited throughout the document, while Section B is the preface providing context and copyright information for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, with Section A referencing external sources that support the technical content introduced in Section B.
Section B references Section A by citing Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, establishing a direct cross-reference for detailed material information.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the quality assurance and material reliability requirements, which are directly applied in Section B to the inspection and classification of DHRS components and valves.
